design_basis: Across two panels, larger proposer pools were useful but unstable. Distributed review repeatedly made useful corrections. The final search batch concentrates review on low-consensus cases and tests stronger specialist routing without granting one judge unilateral authority.

strategies.name: Generalist Vote 15
strategies.description: Frozen repeated control: fifteen independent generalists followed by deterministic plurality.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return the single best complete answer for every case.

strategies.name: Diversified Vote 15
strategies.description: Frozen repeated control: fifteen independently prompted diverse solvers followed by deterministic plurality.

stages.instruction: Solve independently through the assigned lens, but search other allowed families if that lens fails.

strategies.name: E04 Verified Gate
strategies.description: Frozen repeated control: seven proposers, two verifiers, and one judge with a unanimity gate.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and justify one complete allowed mechanism.
Reconstruct the strongest candidates independently and report the answer that survives complete replay.
Choose one exact candidate or a fully checked replacement. Do not average competing tuples.

strategies.name: E04 Proposer Pool
strategies.description: Frozen repeated control: the seven-proposer pool from Experiment 04 with the decision layer removed.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and justify one complete allowed mechanism.

strategies.name: Replicated Sequential Proof Repair
strategies.description: Direct replication of Batch 02's winner: nine proposers, three critics, two verifiers, and a support-gated proof integrator.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return one complete answer.
Identify concrete proposal failures and the strongest repaired answer.
Reconstruct the strongest survivor exactly.
Return the single answer that survives the complete proof audit.

strategies.name: Replicated Large-Base Judge-Free Review
strategies.description: Direct replication of Batch 02's second finalist: ten proposers, two critics, and three weighted verifiers.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and require a complete mechanism.
Identify concrete failures and the strongest repaired answer.
Reconstruct and replay the leading candidates exactly.

strategies.name: Replicated Expanded Judge-Free Review
strategies.description: Replication of the Batch 02 system that made three useful and no harmful corrections from a weak base.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return one exact full-prefix answer.
Falsify weak candidates and return the strongest complete answer.
Independently reconstruct and replay the strongest surviving mechanisms.

strategies.name: Verifier-Strengthened Proof Repair
strategies.description: Eight proposers, three critics, three verifiers, and one integrator shift one call from the base into proof checking.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and report one complete mechanism.
Identify concrete failures and the best repaired answer.
Independently reconstruct the strongest survivor.
Return the single fully replayed answer.

strategies.name: Large Base with Distributed Review
strategies.description: Ten proposers, three critics, and two verifiers use a judge-free weighted decision.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and require a complete mechanism.
Falsify weak candidates and report the strongest complete alternative.
Reconstruct the strongest surviving answer exactly.

strategies.name: Dual-Integrator Large Base
strategies.description: Ten proposers, three verifiers, and two integrators require both integrators plus verifier support to change the base.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and report one exact answer.
Reconstruct the strongest candidates and report the best fully replayed answer.
Independently integrate the evidence and return one exact answer.

strategies.name: Twelve-Solver Weighted Verification
strategies.description: A twelve-proposer base is supplemented by three weighted verifiers without a judge.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and require a full-prefix mechanism.
Independently reconstruct the strongest candidates and report the exact survivor.

strategies.name: Review-Heavy Judge-Free Panel
strategies.description: Eight proposers are balanced by four critics and three verifiers in a distributed weighted decision.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return one exact answer.
Falsify weak candidates and return the strongest complete answer.
Independently reconstruct and replay the strongest survivor.

strategies.name: Low-Consensus Weak-Family Specialists
strategies.description: Five broad solvers route low-consensus cases to ten specialists focused on modular affine and growing-block reconstruction.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and identify the most plausible complete mechanism.

For these low-consensus cases, test two explicit constructions. For MODAFFINE, seek a prime modulus and phase-specific affine maps that replay every transition and wrap. For GROWBLOCK, locate increasing block boundaries, verify each within-block arithmetic progression, and fit the starts and steps. Reject both if another allowed family replays the prefix better.

strategies.name: Low-Consensus Recurrence Specialists
strategies.description: Five broad solvers route low-consensus cases to ten recurrence, stream, and difference specialists.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and identify the most plausible complete mechanism.

For these low-consensus cases, reconstruct periodic affine or second-order recurrences, lagged or interleaved streams, and polynomial differences. Solve parameters from repeated phase transitions and replay the full prefix exactly.

strategies.name: Close-Vote Proof Reserve
strategies.description: Seven solvers handle every case; eight proof verifiers focus only on cases whose top two answers are within two votes.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and report one exact answer.
For close-vote cases only, reconstruct the competing mechanisms and return the exact answer that survives full replay.

strategies.name: Low-Consensus Challenger Reserve
strategies.description: Ten broad solvers route only weak-consensus cases to five independent challengers.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and report one complete answer.
For weak-consensus cases only, independently seek and verify a better complete mechanism.

strategies.name: Four-Agent Router with Eleven Specialists
strategies.description: A small routing layer feeds an eleven-agent specialist bank, maximizing evidence-aware specialist capacity.

stages.instruction: Identify and solve the most plausible complete mechanism while preserving serious alternatives in the concise evidence fields.
Independently solve through the assigned specialty, treating the routing evidence only as hypotheses to test.

strategies.name: Escalating Low-Consensus Deliberation
strategies.description: Six solvers route weak-consensus cases through six challengers and then three integrators.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and report one complete answer.
For weak-consensus cases, construct and test a stronger complete explanation.
Independently integrate the surviving evidence into one exact answer.

strategies.name: Dual Branch with Focused Verification
strategies.description: Two independent five-solver branches form the base; five verifiers focus only on cases where the branches do not converge.

stages.instruction: Solve independently from scratch.
Solve independently from scratch without relying on the first branch.
For cases where the two branches do not converge, reconstruct the strongest candidates exactly.

strategies.name: Weak-Family Balanced Vote 15
strategies.description: Fifteen independent solvers oversample the historically sparse modular, stream, recurrence, and arithmetic lenses without any review hierarchy.

stages.instruction: Solve independently through the assigned specialty. Explicitly reconstruct the full mechanism and reject a specialty-driven explanation that fails any visible term.
